title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Prompt Lens: Repo Conventions
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt repo conventions increment in a coding-agent implementation brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Repo Conventions
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Repo Conventions contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.

content.text: Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt coding edits. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Repo Conventions, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a coding-agent implementation brief.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Generic best practices override project-specific contracts. This fails the Repo Conventions contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Repo Conventions evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-repo-conventions-11j1mx" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Repo Conventions into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-repo-conventions-11j1mx
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Repo Conventions model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Repo Conventions applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
